title: cyanocephalus poliodes
intro: Cyanocephalus poliodes, also known as the "Horned Poppy," is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Papaveraceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits also include its potential to act as an antiseptic, antimicrobial, and anti-hemorrhagic agent, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and heavy menstrual bleeding. Cyanocephalus poliodes can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
